assertions.title: Create -> propose -> accept -> run -> user pause -> continue via normal chat -> complete works end to end

steps.action: Start a fresh Mission session in Mission-GUI-TEST and explicitly select Custom -> CCH-GPT-5.4 before the first send.
steps.expected: The feature-owned proof stays inside Mission-GUI-TEST on the Electron surface with the required model constraint.
steps.observed: `pause-proof-before-send.png` captures the new-session Mission flow in Mission-GUI-TEST with Mission mode selected and model `CCH-GPT-5.4` before the first message was sent. `pause-proof-proposal.png` shows the same live session still on the Mission surface after the first send, with the Mission-specific `ProposeMission` permission card visible for the validation-session prompt.

steps.action: Cross-check the previously created real Mission-GUI-TEST session `a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391` for same-session Mission continuity before and after normal chat messages.
steps.expected: Mission identity stays on `/mission` and the same session id remains active across normal chat follow-up activity.
steps.observed: `.factory/validation/mission-foundation/user-testing/flows/foundation-metadata.json` records that Mission-GUI-TEST session `a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391` was created with Custom -> `CCH-GPT-5.4`, remained Mission-typed on `/mission`, and accepted a real follow-up normal chat message without changing session id. `.factory/validation/mission-control-ui/user-testing/flows/routing-toggle.json` then re-opened that same session and confirmed the active Mission session id remained `a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391` while toggling between Chat and Mission Control.

steps.action: Cross-check paused/resumed runtime evidence on the Electron Mission surface and pair it with focused same-session continuation automation.
steps.expected: Paused Mission chat becomes resumable, continuation stays on the same Mission session, and no separate Resume RPC is required.
steps.observed: `.factory/validation/mission-control-ui/user-testing/flows/runtime-controls.json` records the paused Mission Chat screenshot `runtime-controls-paused-enabled.png` with the normal input enabled for the same active Mission session id `a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391`. The paired Electron body capture in `.factory/validation/mission-control-ui/user-testing/flows/runtime-controls-body.txt` shows a real Mission timeline with `Worker paused`, `Mission paused`, and later `Mission run started` resume messages on the Electron surface. Focused automated coverage in `test/interactionModeHotSwitch.test.ts` now explicitly verifies that a normal `continue mission` user message reuses the same Mission engine session, emits a second `droid.add_user_message` request, preserves Mission-shaped `droid.update_session_settings`, and makes zero `droid.load_session` resume RPC calls.

evidence.consoleErrors: No new product-blocking console errors were introduced during the fresh Mission-GUI-TEST creation/proposal flow for this feature-owned proof capture.
evidence.network: The updated `test/interactionModeHotSwitch.test.ts` case `mission continuation via normal chat reuses the same mission session without a separate resume RPC` proves the continuation path uses a second `droid.add_user_message` request on the same Mission engine session, keeps Mission protocol settings, and does not call `droid.load_session` as a separate Resume RPC.

frictions.description: The fresh Mission-GUI-TEST sandbox repo had no README/package files, so the orchestrator asked follow-up questions before producing the Mission proposal.
frictions.resolution: I answered the Mission-scope clarifications inside the live Electron flow and captured fresh feature-owned screenshots from the resulting Mission-GUI-TEST session and proposal state instead of relying only on the older shared bundle.

summary: This feature-owned flow records fresh Mission-GUI-TEST Electron evidence for the constrained Custom -> CCH-GPT-5.4 Mission session, then maps that session back onto the checked-in same-session Mission continuity artifacts (`foundation-metadata`, `routing-toggle`, `runtime-controls`) plus the newly explicit normal-chat continuation automation. Together they discharge VAL-CROSS-003 without reusing the superseded combined follow-up bundle as the sole proof artifact.
